diagrams.title: BMW Classification Pyramid
diagrams.description: Hierarchical representation showing types of biomedical waste from most to least hazardous
elements: Sharps (very high hazard due to injury + infection)
General waste (bottom - largest volume, least hazardous)
diagrams.purpose: Shows relative hazard levels and volume distribution - helps prioritize handling and disposal resources

diagrams.title: BMW Generation Sources Flowchart
diagrams.description: Flowchart showing waste flow from healthcare facilities to final disposal
elements: Hospitals → Clinics → Labs → Blood banks → Research → Animal facilities → Pharmacies
All converge to: Segregation → Collection → Transportation → Treatment → Disposal
diagrams.purpose: Visualizes how different sources contribute to overall BMW stream and importance of segregation at source

diagrams.title: Colour Coding for BMW Segregation
diagrams.description: Standard colour coding as per BMW Rules 2016 India
elements: Yellow bin: Human anatomical waste, Animal anatomical waste, Soiled waste, Cytotoxic drugs
Red bin: Contaminated plastics, gloves, catheters, tubing
White bin (translucent): Sharps - needles, blades, broken glass
Blue bin: Discarded glassware, Chemical waste, Microbiology waste
diagrams.purpose: Essential for proper segregation at source - prevents mixing of incompatible wastes and ensures appropriate treatment
